text: The discharge from a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher should be directed:

options.text: At the top perimeter layout of smoke clouds
At the base of the flames
Directly into adjacent ventilation return trunks
In wide looping circles across structural partition bulkheads

explanation: The discharge from a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher should always be directed at the base of the flames. This is where the fuel is being vaporized and combustion is occurring, allowing CO2 to displace oxygen and cool the fuel.
